final_output.summary_oneliner: Shell script executes Oracle E-Business Suite patch deployment via SQL*Plus with hardcoded credentials
final_output.summary_complexity: Low complexity single-purpose deployment script with minimal error handling
final_output.summary_risk: High security risk due to hardcoded credentials and lack of validation controls
final_output.functional_purpose: Deploys Oracle E-Business Suite patches by setting APPL_TOP environment and executing SQL deployment script
business_logic: Sets Oracle E-Business Suite application top directory to standard R12.2 path
Connects to database using hardcoded apps schema credentials
Executes deploy_patch.sql script for patch installation
side_effects: Modifies database schema objects through patch deployment
Updates Oracle E-Business Suite metadata and configuration
May restart or invalidate cached application components
functional_inputs: deploy_patch.sql file must exist in current directory
Oracle database must be accessible and running
Apps schema password must match hardcoded value
functional_outputs: SQL*Plus execution results and error messages
Updated database objects per patch contents
Modified Oracle E-Business Suite application configuration
dataflow_steps: Export APPL_TOP environment variable to E-Business Suite path
Initiate SQL*Plus connection to apps schema
Execute deploy_patch.sql script within database session
Return control to shell after script completion

security_issues.description: Hardcoded database credentials expose apps schema password in script and process list
security_issues.evidence: sqlplus apps/apps123 - password visible in script and process list

security_issues.description: No input validation for deploy_patch.sql file existence or content verification
security_issues.evidence: @deploy_patch.sql executed without verification checks

security_issues.description: Missing error handling allows silent failures during patch deployment
security_issues.evidence: No exit code checking or error trapping in script

antipatterns.description: Security vulnerability and maintenance overhead from embedded credentials
antipatterns.recommendation: Use Oracle Wallet or environment variables for credential management
antipatterns.evidence: apps/apps123 credentials embedded in script text
